name: Saratov Oblast (Russia)

colors.symbolism: courage and the historical fields and harvests of the Saratov region
wealth and the wheat-growing tradition (in the coat of arms)

description: A horizontal triband of white, red and blue with a broad central red stripe; the coat of arms of Saratov Oblast (three silver sterlets on a blue shield) is centred on the red stripe.
history: The current flag of Saratov Oblast was adopted on 5 September 1996 and amended later. The arms recall the historical Saratov Governorate and the Volga's importance for the region's fisheries.

features.symbolism: a white-red-blue arrangement with a broad central red field on which the regional coat of arms is placed.
three sterlets (sturgeon-family fish) on an azure shield, the historical arms of Saratov Governorate.
